title: docplan.json
description: Engine-neutral information-architecture plan authored by the content-architect skill and consumed by the endpoint skills (doc-site, readme-author). Hand-authored static JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12), shipped verbatim into target repos beside the emitted docs/docplan.json. Accuracy rule: any claim that cannot be verified against a source in `sources[]` must be recorded in `gaps[]` or a per-document `verification` note, never asserted as fact. Single-mode rule: every DocPlanEntry has exactly one `type` — a document must not mix Diataxis modes. `project.kind` aligns with readme-author's project-kind detection (library | app | cli | framework) plus `service`.

provenance.description: How and when this plan was generated. Network-free; never transmits repo data.

generatedBy.description: Tool that produced the plan, e.g. 'content-architect'.

repoRef.description: Analyzed repo ref (commit or branch) if known.

scope.description: Which documentation spine(s) this plan covers. Routed in Phase 2.

kind.description: Project kind. Aligns with readme-author detection (library | app | cli | framework) plus `service` for internal backend services.

summary.description: One-line description of what the project is.

audiences.description: The reader groups this corpus serves. Referenced by DocPlanEntry.audience.

expertise.description: Assumed reader expertise; drives tone and depth.

sources.description: Ground-truth references discovered during Phase 1 analysis. Referenced by DocPlanEntry.sourceRefs and outline sourceRefs.

type.description: Source category. Tests (especially integration) are the most reliable ground truth.

grouping.description: The corpus's information architecture: ordered sections that map onto doc-site's sidebar model. Array order is sidebar order.

documents.description: Ordered DocPlanEntry ids in this group; order is sidebar order within the group.

gaps.description: Things that could not be verified against ground truth, open questions, and explicit assumptions. The accuracy through-line — must not be silently empty when uncertainty exists.

affects.description: Optional DocPlanEntry ids this gap affects.

DocPlanEntry.description: One planned document. Single-mode invariant: exactly one `type`; a document must not mix Diataxis modes.

slug.description: Route slug (POSIX-style), e.g. 'guides/setup'. `slug` or `path` identifies where the document lands.

path.description: Repo-relative output path, if preferred over a slug.

type.description: The single mode of this document. If family is 'diataxis': tutorial | how-to | reference | explanation. If family is 'architecture': adr | arc42-chapter | c4-view.

subtype.description: Refines an architecture `type`: for c4-view use context | container | component; for arc42-chapter use the chapter name.

purpose.description: A single sentence: the reader need this document serves.

sourceRefs.description: The sources[].id this document is derived from.

intent.description: What this section accomplishes for the reader.

status.description: Lifecycle state. In audit mode, classify existing docs and flag those that need restructuring.

priority.description: Coarse rank so endpoints can stage output (lower = higher priority).

diagrams.description: Prose diagram requests to hand to diagram-generator during drafting. Describe only components verified in source.

verification.description: Per-document note on what was verified against sources vs assumed.

allOf.description: family 'diataxis' ⇒ type is a Diataxis mode.
family 'architecture' ⇒ type is an architecture doc kind.
